emergent_themes.description: Heavy reliance on meal prep services as primary food source, creating a parallel delivery ecosystem outside traditional food delivery apps
Platform loyalty driven by broader ecosystem benefits (transport, travel, loyalty points) rather than food delivery features alone
Deliberate systematization of food to minimize decision-making and cooking, despite understanding of cooking processes
Having subscriptions but unclear understanding of actual benefits received

coding_summary: Rory represents a highly systematized approach to food that prioritizes efficiency over engagement. His meal prep service acts as his primary 'delivery' solution, with traditional food delivery apps serving as weekend treats or social occasions. His platform loyalty to Uber is driven by ecosystem benefits rather than food-specific features, creating high switching barriers. The diary data confirms extensive meal skipping and reliance on systematic solutions when at home. Key insights include the role of meal prep services as delivery competitors, ecosystem lock-in effects, and the gap between subscription ownership and benefit understanding.

coverage_gaps.reason: Limited coverage - participant has unique graduate-to-student transition rather than typical student-to-graduate progression, providing limited insight into standard post-graduation habit evolution
